text: The surgeon demonstrates the mobilization of the descending and sigmoid colon, proceeding with circumferential dissection of the rectum. The dissection begins posteriorly, extending distally, followed by anterior dissection down to the anal canal or pelvic floor, and concludes with the lateral stalks.
The surgeon performs step fourteen, dividing the rectum using total mesorectal excision or tumor-specific mesorectal excision, depending on the tumor's location.
